Appendix: Accessible Ex
The CERC Rhythm (page 13): Crisis communication t
needs and activities evolve through four phases t
in every emergency. The first phase is preparation. in
During preparation communicators should r
draft and test messages, develop partnerships, p
create communication plans, and determine the d
approval process for sending out information in an p
emergency. The second phase is the initial phase. C
During the initial phase of a crisis communicators c
should express empathy, explain risks, promote m
action, and describe response efforts. During the c
third phase, maintenance, communicators need

to explain ongoing risks and will have more time
to segment audiences, providing background
nformation, and addressing rumors. The final phase,
resolution, requires communicators to motivate the
public to stay vigilant and communicators should
discuss lessons learned and revise communication
plans for future emergencies. Throughout all phases,
CERC encourages communicators to engage
communities, empower community members to
make decisions that impact their health, and evaluate
communication efforts.
